2606.06094.title: Integrating Mechanistic and Data-Driven Models for Neurological Disorders through Differentiable Programming
2606.06094.paper_summary: Advances in computational modeling, neuroimaging, and artificial intelligence are revolutionizing the modeling of neurological disorders for improved diagnostics, prognosis, and treatment planning. Mechanistic models provide valuable scientific insight into the disorders, but in practice they are often simplified with assumptions or computationally expensive and slow to solve. However, while purely data driven approaches provide speed and scalability, they require large, high quality data to train and generally suffer from interpretability and generalization issues. This perspective paper presents a structured overview of hybrid modeling strategies, which combine deep learning models with physics based solvers, and are categorized into parallel, series, and parallel-series architectures. Three main approaches that have been emphasized are residual modeling for missing or incomplete physics, Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs) for continuous time dynamics approximation, and solver in the loop that accelerates traditional solvers with neural approximations. These hybrid models integrate the governing differential equation based formulations and deep learning to characterize the evolution of neurological disorders, and promise advanced personalized neurological modeling. In addition, the study explores and proposes different hybrid configurations to improve diagnosis accuracy, predict disease progression, and inform treatment strategies across a range of neurological disorders. These capabilities outperform standalone mechanistic or purely data driven approaches, making hybrid modeling a powerful tool, especially in applications involving modeling the progression and treatment responses in neurological conditions such as brain tumors, Alzheimer's disease, and stroke.
2606.06094.paper_summary_zh: 計算建模、神經影像學和人工智慧的進步正在徹底改變神經系統疾病的建模，以改善診斷、預後和治療計畫。機制模型提供了對這些疾病的寶貴科學見解，但在實踐中，它們通常因假設而被簡化，或計算成本高且求解速度緩慢。然而，雖然純數據驅動的方法提供了速度和可擴展性，但它們需要大量高質量的數據來訓練，並且通常在可解釋性和泛化能力上存在問題。這篇觀點論文提供了一個結構化的混合建模策略概述，這些策略將深度學習模型與基於物理的求解器相結合，並分為並行、串行和並行-串行架構。強調的三種主要方法是針對缺失或不完整物理的殘差建模、用於連續時間動力學近似的神經常微分方程（NODEs），以及通過神經近似加速傳統求解器的求解器在迴圈中。這些混合模型整合了基於微分方程的公式和深度學習，以描述神經系統疾病的演變，並承諾提供先進的個性化神經建模。此外，該研究探索並提出了不同的混合配置，以提高診斷準確性、預測疾病進展，並為一系列神經系統疾病提供治療策略。這些能力超越了獨立的機制或純數據驅動的方法，使混合建模成為一個強大的工具，特別是在涉及建模神經系統疾病進展和治療反應的應用中，例如腦腫瘤、阿茲海默症和中風。
2606.06094.author: Shah Pallav Dhanendrakumar et.al.
2606.06094.authors: Shah Pallav Dhanendrakumar, Saikat Pal, Sitikantha Roy
